newsSinceJun6: June 8: signed MOU with Israel Aerospace Industries granting Palladyne up-to-10-year exclusive U.S. rights to manufacture, integrate and market IAI's combat-proven HARPY, HAROP and Mini-HARPY loitering munitions to the U.S. Department of War; deal requires Palladyne to stand up a U.S. assembly line and pay royalties. Stock nonetheless fell -9.4% on June 9 (and ~24% from its ~$8.60 June 1 level) - a sell-the-news fade reflecting MOU non-bindingness and capex/dilution worries ($43.7M cash vs. assembly-line build-out), not a fundamental break. Q1 backdrop: revenue +107% YoY to $3.5M, ~$17M backlog, FY26 guide $24-27M reiterated.

sources.label: Business Wire - Palladyne AI and IAI partnership announcement (Jun 8, 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new evidence since yesterday's down-move: the June 8 IAI MOU directly confirms this page's defense-autonomy thesis (embodied AI, small-cap optionality), while a -9.4% sell-the-news fade unwinds the YTD rerating (+52% -> +38%) that drove yesterday's penalty. Thesis stronger, price lower, crowding reduced - residual alpha improves on this page.
verdicts.bull: Exclusive up-to-10-year U.S. rights to combat-proven IAI loitering munitions gives a $350M-cap name a tangible path into the DoD munitions replenishment cycle, layered on top of 107% YoY revenue growth and ~$17M backlog.
verdicts.neutral: It is an MOU, not a contract: revenue depends on standing up a U.S. assembly line and winning Department of War orders, and FY26 guidance of $24-27M was merely reiterated, so near-term numbers do not change.
verdicts.bear: The pivot drifts further from robotics/AI software toward licensed munitions manufacturing, diluting page purity, and the assembly-line capex against $43.7M cash makes an equity raise into weakness a live risk.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Conversion of the IAI MOU into a definitive agreement and a first U.S. Department of War loitering-munitions order.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Dilutive capital raise to fund the U.S. assembly line, or the non-binding MOU stalling without orders.
